suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document an explicit commit-inspection variant of the replay procedure for release-tag milestones. Currently the skill is written around PR-oriented workflows (gh pr view, gh pr diff), but release-tag milestones need gh api repos/.../commits/<sha> instead. Making this a first-class option would avoid followedProcedure=false for valid release-tag replays.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature r6931-healthz-head worker had to deviate from the pr-replay-worker procedure to use commit-based inspection via gh api instead of gh pr view. Similarly, r6931-auth-refresh-backoff and r6931-codex-stream-output are release-tag milestones that would need the same adaptation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Make the reviewer fallback (invoking the review skill directly when agent-browser is unavailable) a documented first-class option rather than a deviation. Multiple release-tag milestone workers have hit this constraint.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature r6931-healthz-head worker reported followedProcedure=false due to the reviewer fallback deviation. This is an environment constraint that applies broadly to Windows-based sessions.
